title: Onramp plan: dedicated fleet sizing + vendor timing for Nimbus deal
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Garcia <elena@nimbusanalytics.com>, Maxwell Reed <max.reed@proximaglobal.com>, sales@astracloud.com
Date: 2026-10-20T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Onramp plan: dedicated fleet sizing + vendor timing for Nimbus deal

Team — looping you in on timing and vendor asks for Nimbus (DEAL-4782). They signed verbally last week; legal is wrapping final terms but they want a dedicated pool in us-west with ~8x A100 80GB-equivalent units for the first 12 months, with autoscale to add 4 more if usage ramps.

Primary asks:
- Confirm an accurate sizing recommendation for initial commit (Karthik).
- Get OEM quote lead times and expedited shipping options (Marissa to coordinate with Maxwell / Proxima).
- Check reserved instance / committed capacity pricing and earliest start date from cloud provider (Lauren to confirm with AstraCloud).
- Align on contract milestone that should trigger procurement (Ben).

I've attached a quick sizing workbook (nimbus_capacity_sizing.xlsx) with their expected qps and 99th latency targets. We're optimistic we can start serving on Redis-prefetch + KV caching pattern which reduces GPU need, but they want guaranteed capacity behind an SLA for enterprise compliance.

I'll host a 30-min sync Wed 10/27 to agree on a procurement path. Please review the sheet and reply with any critical gaps.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Exec, Redwood Inference
-----

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Garcia <elena@nimbusanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-22T11:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onramp plan: dedicated fleet sizing + vendor timing for Nimbus deal

Ben / team — reviewed their traffic profiles. Quick notes:
- Baseline: 8 x A100-80GB equivalent gives headroom for steady-state throughput (~120 qps with our batching) and meets 99th latency under typical prompt sizes.
- Burn rate: with caching and 2s avg prompt, expected token/sec profile suggests burst headroom needed; recommend keeping +2 spare GPUs (10 total) for initial 30 days.
- Autoscale: need cloud provider support for rapid attach (hot-add). If cloud cannot hot-add within 10 minutes, we should provision 2 on standby on Dedicated node-level failover.

Action items for procurement planning:
- OEM route (purchase hardware) lead time 10–14 weeks for custom racks; 6–8 weeks if we accept standard config.
- Cloud reserved capacity (AstraCloud): early estimates from layer indicate reservable capacity in 2–4 weeks but depends on region occupancy.

I've updated the sizing workbook with two scenarios (conservative vs optimized). Attaching notes in the sheet.

—Karthik
Principal Solutions Engineer
-----

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Maxwell Reed <max.reed@proximaglobal.com>, sales@astracloud.com, Elena Garcia <elena@nimbusanalytics.com>
Date: 2026-10-26T08:32:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onramp plan: dedicated fleet sizing + vendor timing for Nimbus deal

Thanks — quick vendor updates after my calls this morning:
- Proxima (Maxwell): can supply 10x A100-80GB-equivalent nodes in 6 weeks if we accept their standard rack and sign a 36-month maintenance addendum. Expedited shipping adds ~10% premium. They've requested our exact rack spec sheet — I attached OEM_quote_request_Redwood.docx for their intake. Maxwell will provide an initial PO-ready quote by 11/05.
- AstraCloud (cloud reservations): sales rep says region us-west has limited reserved pool; they can guarantee 8 reserved units with start date 12/01 if we commit by 11/15. Hot-add latency depends on fleet fragmentation; worst-case 20 minutes for provisioning new GPUs during peaks.

Given Karthik's recommendation (+2 spare), we either:
A) Commit to AstraCloud reserved 8 + 2 on-demand burst (fastest go-live, 12/01 start but risk 20-min scale-up), or
B) Place OEM order for 10 units (6 weeks) and keep cloud burst for overflow — OEM earliest ready ~12/10 with expedited.

Elena — which of the two options would you prefer from an ops/contract standpoint? We can negotiate a phased SLA that starts when cloud capacity is reserved and transitions to dedicated OEM hardware when delivered.

I'll own vendor follow-up and gather formal quotes. Proposed procurement trigger = signature + initial payment milestone; recommend syncing legal to align payment milestone with delivery windows.

Marissa
Senior Sales Director
Redwood Inference
Attachment: OEM_quote_request_Redwood.docx
-----

From: Elena Garcia <elena@nimbusanalytics.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-30T14:42:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Onramp plan: dedicated fleet sizing + vendor timing for Nimbus deal

Hi all — quick decision from our side after internal ops check. We want the fastest path to production with predictable SLA for our customers. Preference: Option A (AstraCloud reserved 8 + 2 on-demand burst) to hit a 12/01 start, with staged transition to OEM hardware once available (we're fine with a hardware cutover by mid-December if that shortens long-term TCO).

Two requests before we finalize: 
1) Please include clear fallback policies (region failover + model variant) and a runbook for handling hot-add failure modes.
2) We need a firm delivery/installation SLA for the OEM pieces and a warranty/maintenance summary.

Ben/Marissa — please send the vendor quotes and proposed milestone language. We will sign the order and wire initial payment conditioned on the reservation confirmation and the runbook.

Regards,
Elena Garcia
Head of Platform Engineering
Nimbus Analytics

---
Notes: attachments referenced but included as filename stubs. Thread contains quoted replies and typical vendor coordination. HubSpot deal link and DocuSign placeholder added in metadata for procurement flow.

title: Dedicated capacity sizing: Praxis AI — sync & calendar confirmation
preview:
From: Amelia Ross <amelia@praxis.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-27T09:12:00-05:00
Subject: Interest in Dedicated capacity — quick sync?

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick product — we kicked the tires with a few self-serve runs and it looks promising. We're evaluating a Dedicated deployment for latency-critical features in our inference path. High-level needs:
- Peak concurrent requests: ~600 qps (burst to ~900)
- Typical prompt length: 450 tokens; want 95th pctile token latency <= 220ms
- Data isolation and audit logs are must-haves

Can we set up a short call to go over Dedicated sizing, expected throughput, and what tuning looks like for latency-heavy workloads? I'm including Mark (CTO) on future threads.

Best,
Amelia
Amelia Ross | Head of ML Infrastructure
Praxis AI




From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Amelia Ross <amelia@praxis.ai>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-27T11:03:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Interest in Dedicated capacity — quick sync?

Hi Amelia — great to meet you over email. I'm Ben (SDR) at Redwood Inference. Thanks for the context — that traffic profile is exactly the kind of workload our Dedicated offering is built for.

title: Lyricare: SAML assertion exceeds limit prevents supplier invite acceptance
preview:
From: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@lyricare.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Nicole Park <nicole.park@lyricare.com>
Date: 2026-11-28T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Can't accept supplier invite - SAML assertion size error

Hi Ben,

We tried to accept the Coupa invite your team sent yesterday but are getting an error in our IdP (Okta) and the portal shows a failure page: \"SAML assertion size exceeds configured limit\". We captured a SAML trace — attaching as saml-trace-lyricare.txt — and a screenshot of the invite error.

We have a PO pending (PO-22987) that needs the vendor activated by EOD Friday. Can you confirm whether there's an alternate flow (manual provisioning or one-time activation link) while we investigate our IdP attributes?

Thanks,
Tom Hsu
IT Manager, Lyricare Inc.
T: +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: saml-trace-lyricare.txt (text), invite-error.png (image)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@lyricare.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-28T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Can't accept supplier invite - SAML assertion size error

Hi Tom — thanks for the details and the trace. Copying Marissa from Ops (billing/onboarding) and Jonas from Engineering — we'll get this triaged.

Quick summary of options while we investigate the SAML assertion size:
- Manual vendor provisioning: we can create a vendor record on your behalf in the supplier portal and send a one-time activation link that bypasses the SAML acceptance flow. This preserves the PO flow and is the fastest path.
- Short-term configuration change: if Lyricare can trim large group attributes (we see a lot of role/group claims in the trace) that often fixes the assertion size.
- Long-term: we'll add a more robust handling path in our onboarding service for oversized assertions, but that requires an engineering change (Jonas can estimate).

Please send the saml-trace (you did — thanks) and confirm whether we should proceed with manual provisioning now. If yes, share the contact name and email for the vendor admin at Lyricare to receive the activation link.

I'm creating JIRA-ONB-8742 to track this.

— Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 510-555-0199

From: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@lyricare.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Nicole Park <nicole.park@lyricare.com>
Date: 2026-11-28T11:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Can't accept supplier invite - SAML assertion size error

Thanks Ben — attaching the full SAML trace (same file referenced above) — the IdP team confirms that our Okta is currently sending ~120 group claims for certain partner accounts. We can reduce the group claim set but it will take 2–3 business days to update mapping across our environment.

If you can manual-provision for now, please send the activation to nicole.park@lyricare.com (she's the person handling vendor onboarding here). We also need the vendor record to show the Redwood vendor ID and have the PO (PO-22987) linked so our AP team can process payment.

Appreciate the quick help.
Tom

Attachments: saml-trace-lyricare.txt (text)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@lyricare.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T16:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Can't accept supplier invite - SAML assertion size error

On Nov 28, 2026, Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@lyricare.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — attaching the full SAML trace ...

Tom — we went ahead with manual provisioning as requested. Steps taken:
- Created a vendor record for Lyricare Inc. in the supplier portal, vendor ID RW-77821. Screenshot attached.
- Linked PO-22987 to the vendor record so AP visibility is immediate.
- Sent a one-time activation link to nicole.park@lyricare.com. The link expires in 48 hours.

Attachment: manual-provision-instructions.pdf (PDF) — contains instructions for Nicole on how to finish the activation and where to upload necessary tax documents.

Next steps from our side (tracking in JIRA-ONB-8742):
- Jonas to evaluate assertion parsing and whether we can accept large assertions by streaming/truncation in the SAML helper (ETA ~10 business days to scope + changes).
- Marissa to confirm vendor record fields and billing metadata with Lyricare.

Please confirm once Nicole has accepted the link so I can close the urgent PO blocker.

Best,
Ben

From: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@lyricare.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-30T08:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Can't accept supplier invite - SAML assertion size error

Ben — Nicole accepted the link at 08:30 and she uploaded the W-9 and bank routing instructions. Payment team can see RW-77821 now and will proceed with PO-22987. Thank you for the fast turnaround.

One question: what's the timeline for the permanent fix you mentioned (supporting oversize assertions)? We'd prefer not to have to change our Okta mappings because it's used across many integrations.

Tom
IT Manager, Lyricare Inc.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@lyricare.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-30T12:10:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Can't accept supplier invite - SAML assertion size error

Great to hear Nicole got it completed — thanks for confirming.

Jonas and I discussed options and here's the short plan:
- Immediate: keep the manual-provision fallback documented (we've updated the partner onboarding playbook and attached the manual provision instructions to the JIRA ticket).
- Short-term engineering change: Jonas will add a streaming/parsing path in the SAML helper to handle larger signed assertions without failing outright. We estimate 2 weeks for a scoped fix + 1 week for QA and rollout (total ~3 weeks).
- Long-term: update our partner docs with recommended IdP mappings to reduce unnecessary group/role claims (non-blocking if engineering fix is applied).

If Lyricare prefers us to prioritize the code change, Jonas can dedicate additional cycles and we can tighten the ETA. Would you like us to do that? Happy to set a 30-minute call with your IdP team and Jonas to align.

I'll keep this ticket open and follow up with status updates.

— Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

title: Coordinating 30-min product briefing — stakeholder time pairs
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Turner <sophie.turner@brightwaveai.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Coordinating 30-min product briefing — stakeholder time pairs

Hi Sophie —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and next steps so we keep momentum:

- Recap: You’re exploring an inference platform to reduce per-token cost and guarantee sub-200ms median latency for your customer-facing chat assistant. Primary constraints: multi-tenant security model, on-prem/private deployment for regulated datasets, and predictable burst capacity.
- Ask: For the 30-minute intro/demo we’ll tailor the flow to the stakeholders you bring. To be most useful, please confirm whether you want a broad product tour or a focused look at the private deployment / security controls.

Proposed stakeholder time pairs (two options to make calendar alignment easier):

Option A — Product + Platform
- Tue Dec 6, 2026 — 09:00–09:30 PT (Avery + Ben)

Option B — Product + Security
- Wed Dec 7, 2026 — 11:30–12:00 PT (Avery + Ben + Marcus for a short security dig-in)

Fallback window: Dec 8–10, late morning PT preferred.

Attachments: Briefing-Agenda.pdf (short agenda), Pre-demo-questionnaire.docx (3 quick qual Qs we use to tailor the demo).

Quick qual for the demo prep — if you can reply with the bullets below, we’ll adjust the emphasis:
- Expected concurrent users / reqs per second (approx.)
- Data residency / retention concerns (PII, logs)
- Any blocked IP ranges or SSO/SAML notes we should know in advance

If you confirm stakeholders + pick A or B I’ll send an invite and prep doc.

Thanks —
Avery Johnson
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
avery@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0129

Redwood Inference — Make inference predictable and economical

Subject: Re: Coordinating 30-min product briefing — stakeholder time pairs

> Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and next steps so we keep momentum:

Thanks, Avery — this looks good. We’re most interested in a focused demo on private deployment + security controls (that’s the highest lift for us right now).

We can do Option B: Wed Dec 7, 11:30–12:00 PT. I’ve looped in Mark Huang (Head of Security) to the thread — he’ll join for the security portion. A few quick answers to your prep Qs:
- Expected throughput: target ~200 req/s during steady state, bursts to ~600 for short periods.
- Data residency: must retain audit logs for 12 months; PII must be redacted or tokenized at ingress.
- SSO: We use Okta; we’ll share SAML metadata ahead of time if we move to a Private deployment.

Please include any pre-demo checklist we should fill out — happy to get the questionnaire back to you today.

Best,

Hi Sophie —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and next steps so we keep momentum:

- Recap: You’re exploring an inference platform to reduce per-token cost and guarantee sub-200ms median latency for your customer-facing chat assistant. Primary constraints: multi-tenant security model, on-prem/private deployment for regulated datasets, and predictable burst capacity.
- Ask: For the 30-minute intro/demo we’ll tailor the flow to the stakeholders you bring. To be most useful, please confirm whether you want a broad product tour or a focused look at the private deployment / security controls.

Proposed stakeholder time pairs (two options to make calendar alignment easier):

Option A — Product + Platform
- Tue Dec 6, 2026 — 09:00–09:30 PT (Avery + Ben)

Option B — Product + Security
- Wed Dec 7, 2026 — 11:30–12:00 PT (Avery + Ben + Marcus for a short security dig-in)

Fallback window: Dec 8–10, late morning PT preferred.

Attachments: Briefing-Agenda.pdf (short agenda), Pre-demo-questionnaire.docx (3 quick qual Qs we use to tailor the demo).

Quick qual for the demo prep — if you can reply with the bullets below, we’ll adjust the emphasis:
- Expected concurrent users / reqs per second (approx.)
- Data residency / retention concerns (PII, logs)
- Any blocked IP ranges or SSO/SAML notes we should know in advance

Thanks —
Avery Johnson

avery@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0129

title: Crossroads Health activation charter & owner demarcation
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwoodai.com>
To: Matthew Long <matthew@crossroadshealth.com>, Julia Tan <julia@crossroadshealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwoodai.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwoodai.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Crossroads Health activation charter & owner demarcation

Hi Matthew / Julia —

Thanks again for signing — really excited to kick this off. Attached is a first-pass activation charter and a lightweight mutual action plan (MAP) that lays out the sprint-0 scope, acceptance criteria, and owner suggestions. Key things to call out up-front:

- Goal: pilot Redwood inference for the EHR summarization flow (internal-facing, HIPAA consented) with a goal to hit 95% reliability under the agreed latency SLO during pilot.
- Scope for sprint-0 (2 weeks): network & VPC connectivity validation, model selection + baseline perf run, and a first integration via our hosted API.
- Acceptance: successful tokenized generation at test rate (see MAP tab "acceptance") + infra sign-off from Crossroads infra.

Please confirm that the network contacts below are correct and that the proposed dates in the MAP work for you. I’ve CC’d Priya (implementation lead) and Lauren (CS manager) from our side — they’ll take over the detailed scheduling and runbook creation once we align on the sprint-0 window.

Attachments: CR_CSP_MutualActionPlan_v1.xlsx, crossroads_network_requirements.pdf

Next steps:
1) Matthew/Julia: confirm network contact and availability for the week of Dec 7 for sprint-0 kick.
2) Priya: prep a short internal checklist (VPN keys, sample payloads, test corpus) and flag any pre-reqs we need from Crossroads infra.

Thanks — I’ll wait for your OK before we schedule the kickoff calendar invite.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex@redwoodai.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

--
Redwood Inference — Production-first LLM inference for dev teams

Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwoodai.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 09:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Crossroads Health activation charter & owner demarcation

Thanks Alex — this looks good. A couple quick clarifications from our side:

- Our infra contact is eng-infra@crossroadshealth.com (I copied them above). For VPN/testing we'd prefer Dec 8–10 rather than the week of Dec 7 if that’s possible.
- On acceptance: can you confirm whether the acceptance tests will require any PHI in the payloads or whether you have a synthetic dataset we can use? Our security team will not allow real PHI for the pilot connectivity tests.
- Who on your side is the primary SME for model perf (for the baseline runs)? That person will need access to our staging endpoints for perf tuning.

If you can answer the above, we’ll block Dec 8–10 tentatively and loop in security for a quick review.

Thanks,
Julia

Crossroads Health

title: Contingency & Cross-Region Recovery Capabilities — Starlite RFP
preview:
From: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@starlitebank.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@starlitebank.com
Date: 2026-11-20T09:12:00-05:00
Subject: Starlite DR questionnaire — initial submission

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick onboarding call earlier. Attached is Starlite's standard disaster recovery questionnaire focused on RTO/RPO, multi-region failover sequencing, and test cadence. Our procurement window is tight — we are targeting a contract decision by mid-December.

Key items we need answered in detail:
- Maximum supported RTO and RPO per workload class (auth, session, long-running inference)
- Multi-region replication model (active-active, active-passive, warm standby) and expected failover time
- Data residency implications when failing between regions, encryption-at-rest and in-transit details, and KMS options
- Failback procedures and data reconciliation expectations
- Frequency and scope of DR tests and whether customers can observe test runs
- Any impacts to cost when keeping warm standby capacity vs. on-demand recovery

I've attached Starlite_DR_Questionnaire.pdf. Please route responses to me and cc procurement. If you need more time for technical appendices, let us know a realistic delivery date.

Appreciate it —
Oliver
Senior Manager, Infrastructure Risk
Starlite Bank
[Attachment: Starlite_DR_Questionnaire.pdf (PDF, 210KB)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@starlitebank.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-20T11:03:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Starlite DR questionnaire — initial submission

Oliver —

Thanks for sending the questionnaire and for the call this morning. We've started compiling answers and have attached a preliminary response covering the high-level RTO/RPO profiles and our standard failover approach.

Quick notes up front:
- Redwood supports workload-class RTOs ranging from ~30s (for cached routing/edge auth paths) to 30 minutes for large-batch offline jobs; RPOs are typically sub-second for real-time services and up to 15 minutes for batch-processing tiers. We'll include the precise numbers per workload in the attached doc.
- Our multi-region approach for Hosted accounts is active-passive with automated health-driven routing and automated cache rebuilds. For Dedicated and Private deployments we support warm-standby or active-active depending on customer configuration.
- We can provide SOC 2 evidence via a secure link if required — will confirm the exact access method in the follow-up.

Laura and Karthik are taking point on the technical appendix and the failover runbook details. Expect the next, more detailed message from Laura with architecture diagrams and the test cadence matrix.

Attached: Redwood_Prelim_DR_Response.docx

Regards,
Ben
Director, Customer Solutions — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood_Prelim_DR_Response.docx (DOCX, 120KB)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@starlitebank.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-23T08:22:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Starlite DR questionnaire — technical appendix and failover matrix

Oliver —

Following up with the technical appendix that maps your questionnaire to our operational design. Summary highlights below; please see the attached failover matrix and a public runbook link for deeper flow diagrams.

1) RTO / RPO by workload class- Auth/session routing (edge cache): RTO target = 30 seconds; RPO = 0s (stateless tokens + cache rehydration)- Real-time model inference (thin-server GPU endpoints): RTO target = 2 minutes for APIs with active cache; RPO = ~1 second using replication of request metadata and model artifacts in KV cache snapshots- Batch/analytics jobs: RTO = 30 minutes (requeue and replay), RPO = 15 minutes (suitable for non-customer-facing batch jobs)

2) Multi-region replication model- Hosted: active-passive with continuous asynchronous replication of object storage and near-real-time replication of metadata. Route failover via DNS + health-checked regional gateway. Typical service-level failover time (DNS propagation + warm-up) = 90-180 seconds for API traffic when using our smart-routing TTL optimization.- Dedicated/Private: options for warm-standby (hot pools reserved) or active-active (cross-region sharding) depending on contract. Warm-standby achieve failover within 60-120 seconds assuming reserved pool is configured.

3) Data residency & encryption- All customer data encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256). For Private deployments we support customer-managed keys (bring-your-own-KMS/HSM). Hosted customers may use Redwood-managed KMS by default; we can enable CMK for enterprise accounts.

4) Failback & reconciliation- Failback is orchestrated with consistent snapshot checkpoints; cross-region writes during a failover window are reconciled via write-ahead logs with deterministic ordering where required. For active-passive flows, write-forwarding is disabled to avoid split-brain. Reconciliation usually completes within the RPO window; we include pre-and-post integrity checks and checksums.

5) Test cadence and customer visibility- Quarterly full failover exercises for enterprise customers (can be increased to monthly on request). We run tabletop exercises monthly with customer ops; full end-to-end failovers are scheduled quarterly and customers can observe via a secure session and read-only access to the test run logs. We provide test reports with timestamps and remediation items.

6) Cost implications- Warm standby requires reserved GPU pool capacity; we provide two profiles (low-cost warm: minimal instance types; full-warm: capacity-sized to production). We include an impact table in the attached spreadsheet showing estimated monthly incremental cost.

Attachments: redwood-swiftfailover-matrix.xlsx
Runbook: https://redwood-internal.example.com/runbooks/region-failover-v2

Let me know which workloads Starlite considers mission-critical and we'll map exact instance sizing and reserve recommendations.

—Laura
Sr. Product Manager, Reliability
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: redwood-swiftfailover-matrix.xlsx (XLSX, 95KB)]

From: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@starlitebank.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, starlite-enterprise-security@starlitebank.com
Date: 2026-11-25T10:45:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Starlite DR questionnaire — follow-up questions

Laura / Ben —

Thanks for the detailed appendix — very helpful. A few clarifying items from our security and procurement teams:

1) KMS / Key ownership: For the Private deployment option, can you confirm the exact HSM/KMS vendors you support (AWS KMS CMK, Azure Key Vault, on-prem HSM)? Our security team prefers customer-controlled keys with strict key rotation policies.
2) Warm standby billing: When warm standby is reserved but unused for long periods, do you offer burstable pricing or credits for infrequent failovers? We need to understand how your pricing model treats idle reserved GPUs during non-disaster windows.
3) SOC 2 and test evidence: Please provide a secure link to your SOC 2 Type II report and clarify whether pen-test reports / third-party DR attestation are available for enterprise review under an NDA.

Also, can you confirm whether Redwood's warm-standby plan supports a manual failback window (i.e., operator-initiated failback) versus automatic failback? We have internal change-control constraints that prefer scheduled manual failback.

Attached: Starlite_DR_Followup.pdf

Oliver
Senior Manager, Infrastructure Risk
Starlite Bank
[Attachment: Starlite_DR_Followup.pdf (PDF, 45KB)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@starlitebank.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-29T16:07:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Starlite DR questionnaire — final clarifications and next steps

Oliver —

Appreciate the follow-up. Answers inline and next steps below: 

1) KMS / Key ownership- We support AWS KMS (CMK), Azure Key Vault (BYOK), and common on-prem HSM integrations for Private deployments. Customer-managed keys and rotation policies can be enforced via our key-management connectors; we will include the configuration checklist in the security appendix.

2) Warm standby billing- We offer two commercial patterns: (A) reserved warm: fixed monthly reserved capacity (lower unit price) and (B) on-demand failover: no reserved capacity but longer RTO while instances boot (costs only incurred on failover). For long-lived reserved pools we provide a usage credit program that applies credits for long idle periods if usage thresholds are not crossed — negotiable under enterprise contracts. Procurement (Karthik) can model both options and include credit scenarios in the SOW.

3) SOC 2 and attestations- We will provide a secure download link for our SOC 2 Type II report and last two penetration test executive summaries under the standard NDA. Link below is to the SOC 2 material; we'll provision access to the pen-test files after NDA execution.

4) Failback mode- Manual, operator-initiated failback is supported and is our recommended default for customers with strict change control. We can also enable automated failback but we disable it by default for Dedicated and Private deployments to avoid unintended rollbacks.

Next steps:
- We will send the SOC2 link and the security appendix by COB tomorrow.
- Karthik, will follow up with a cost comparison for reserved-warm vs on-demand failover and a proposed SOW clause for standby credits.
- Proposed call: 2026-12-02 at 10:00 AM ET to walk through the SOC2 access and to review the failover matrix together. Please confirm availability.

Thanks — we'll be ready to provide live runbook walkthrough during the call if helpful.

Ben Carter
Director, Customer Solutions — Redwood Inference
[SECURE LINK: https://secure.redwood.example.com/soc2/download?token=fd3e9]

To: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@starlitebank.com>

Date: 2026-11-20T11:03:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Starlite DR questionnaire — initial submission

Oliver —

Thanks for sending the questionnaire and for the call this morning. We've started compiling answers and have attached a preliminary response covering the high-level RTO/RPO profiles and our standard failover approach.

Quick notes up front:
- Redwood supports workload-class RTOs ranging from ~30s (for cached routing/edge auth paths) to 30 minutes for large-batch offline jobs; RPOs are typically sub-second for real-time services and up to 15 minutes for batch-processing tiers. We'll include the precise numbers per workload in the attached doc.
- Our multi-region approach for Hosted accounts is active-passive with automated health-driven routing and automated cache rebuilds. For Dedicated and Private deployments we support warm-standby or active-active depending on customer configuration.
- We can provide SOC 2 evidence via a secure link if required — will confirm the exact access method in the follow-up.

Laura and Karthik are taking point on the technical appendix and the failover runbook details. Expect the next, more detailed message from Laura with architecture diagrams and the test cadence matrix.

Attached: Redwood_Prelim_DR_Response.docx

Regards,
Ben
Director, Customer Solutions — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood_Prelim_DR_Response.docx (DOCX, 120KB)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@starlitebank.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-23T08:22:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Starlite DR questionnaire — technical appendix and failover matrix

Oliver —

Following up with the technical appendix that maps your questionnaire to our operational design. Summary highlights below; please see the attached failover matrix and a public runbook link for deeper flow diagrams.

1) RTO / RPO by workload class- Auth/session routing (edge cache): RTO target = 30 seconds; RPO = 0s (stateless tokens + cache rehydration)- Real-time model inference (thin-server GPU endpoints): RTO target = 2 minutes for APIs with active cache; RPO = ~1 second using replication of request metadata and model artifacts in KV cache snapshots- Batch/analytics jobs: RTO = 30 minutes (requeue and replay), RPO = 15 minutes (suitable for non-customer-facing batch jobs)

2) Multi-region replication model- Hosted: active-passive with continuous asynchronous replication of object storage and near-real-time replication of metadata. Route failover via DNS + health-checked regional gateway. Typical service-level failover time (DNS propagation + warm-up) = 90-180 seconds for API traffic when using our smart-routing TTL optimization.- Dedicated/Private: options for warm-standby (hot pools reserved) or active-active (cross-region sharding) depending on contract. Warm-standby achieve failover within 60-120 seconds assuming reserved pool is configured.

3) Data residency & encryption- All customer data encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256). For Private deployments we support customer-managed keys (bring-your-own-KMS/HSM). Hosted customers may use Redwood-managed KMS by default; we can enable CMK for enterprise accounts.

4) Failback & reconciliation- Failback is orchestrated with consistent snapshot checkpoints; cross-region writes during a failover window are reconciled via write-ahead logs with deterministic ordering where required. For active-passive flows, write-forwarding is disabled to avoid split-brain. Reconciliation usually completes within the RPO window; we include pre-and-post integrity checks and checksums.

5) Test cadence and customer visibility- Quarterly full failover exercises for enterprise customers (can be increased to monthly on request). We run tabletop exercises monthly with customer ops; full end-to-end failovers are scheduled quarterly and customers can observe via a secure session and read-only access to the test run logs. We provide test reports with timestamps and remediation items.

6) Cost implications- Warm standby requires reserved GPU pool capacity; we provide two profiles (low-cost warm: minimal instance types; full-warm: capacity-sized to production). We include an impact table in the attached spreadsheet showing estimated monthly incremental cost.

Attachments: redwood-swiftfailover-matrix.xlsx
Runbook: https://redwood-internal.example.com/runbooks/region-failover-v2

Let me know which workloads Starlite considers mission-critical and we'll map exact instance sizing and reserve recommendations.

—Laura
Sr. Product Manager, Reliability
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: redwood-swiftfailover-matrix.xlsx (XLSX, 95KB)]

Oliver —

Appreciate the follow-up. Answers inline and next steps below: 

1) KMS / Key ownership- We support AWS KMS (CMK), Azure Key Vault (BYOK), and common on-prem HSM integrations for Private deployments. Customer-managed keys and rotation policies can be enforced via our key-management connectors; we will include the configuration checklist in the security appendix.

2) Warm standby billing- We offer two commercial patterns: (A) reserved warm: fixed monthly reserved capacity (lower unit price) and (B) on-demand failover: no reserved capacity but longer RTO while instances boot (costs only incurred on failover). For long-lived reserved pools we provide a usage credit program that applies credits for long idle periods if usage thresholds are not crossed — negotiable under enterprise contracts. Procurement (Karthik) can model both options and include credit scenarios in the SOW.

4) Failback mode- Manual, operator-initiated failback is supported and is our recommended default for customers with strict change control. We can also enable automated failback but we disable it by default for Dedicated and Private deployments to avoid unintended rollbacks.

title: Re: HelixHealth pilot — subprocessor certification path & TIA rollback controls
preview:
From: Samuel Gray <samuel.gray@helixhealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: HelixHealth Legal <legal@helixhealth.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0500
Subject: Subprocessor attestation + TIA rollback controls for UK pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. Our legal & privacy team has a few follow-ups before we can move the pilot into production in the UK:

1) Subprocessor certification: We need a list of any third-party subprocessors involved in request routing, on-prem connectors, and logging/exfiltration tools, plus a short attestation from each that they can comply with the UK Addendum (IDTA) security obligations.

2) TIA rollback controls: If we rely on Transitional Impact Assessments (TIAs) for an interim routing path, HelixHealth needs explicit rollback controls — i.e., how you'd revert to onshore-only processing if an issue is detected, and how quickly (SLA) that full rollback happens. Also, evidence of testing this rollback (runbook or test results) would be ideal.

3) Evidence handoff cadence: we prefer a staged evidence handoff (initial attestation within 5 business days, full artifacts within 3 weeks). Can you confirm whether Redwood can meet that cadence and whether any items will be redacted for security reasons?

I attached the HelixHealth questionnaire we use to capture subprocessor info. Can you route to your security/compliance team and let us know next steps?

Best,
Sam

Attachment: helix_subprocessor_questionnaire.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samuel Gray <samuel.gray@helixhealth.com>
Cc: HelixHealth Legal <legal@helixhealth.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Subprocessor attestation + TIA rollback controls for UK pilot

Hi Sam —

Thanks for the clear list. We're aligned on the ask and can meet the cadence you proposed with one caveat (see \"redactions\" below). Here's what I propose as an action plan: 

- Day 1 (today): Acknowledgement and initial point-of-contact (this email).
- Day 3: Subprocessor roster + short-form attestations (we'll provide names, roles, and attestation statements).
- Day 14: Full attestation artifacts, technical excerpts (network flow diagrams, key rotation schedules) and a documented rollback runbook.
- Day 21: Evidence of rollback test (redacted test logs + vendor test summary).

Redactions: Some vendor test logs include infrastructure IPs and internal ticket IDs. We can provide redacted logs that still show the test results and timestamps. Anything we can't share will be summarized by our security engineer and flagged for HelixHealth Legal to review.

TIA rollback controls (summary):
- Rollback trigger criteria: degraded routing to onshore (<95% success for 10 mins), cryptographic verification failures, or vendor attestation revocation.
- Rollback path: Automatic routing policy change within our smart router + immediate KV cache flush for affected sessions.
- Target rollback time: automated fallback within 6 minutes; full traffic verification and confirmation within 2 hours (manual sign-off).
- Test evidence: We'll run a staged rollback test in our staging environment and provide a test packet trace and short test report.

I've looped in Naomi (Security) and Kimberly (Compliance) to coordinate the attestation drafts. We'll aim to upload the initial packages to the Drive link below.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1a2b3cHelixEvidence

Cheers,
Ava Chen
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference

--
Ava Chen
Director, Solutions
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Kimberly, please confirm whether we require an executed MoU before submitting the full artifacts.

2) Attestation language: We should use a standard short-form template that maps directly to Section 5 of the Redwood DPA + UK Addendum clauses 3.1–3.5. Marissa, can you draft the short-form attestation and include a checkbox matrix for key commitments (encryption at rest/in transit, access logs, breach notification timeline)?

3) Rollback test artifacts: We will not share raw packet captures. Instead, we'll provide a redacted packet timeline and a cryptographic checksum of a sample trace (so Helix can verify the sample without seeing payloads). Also include the runbook steps and test time windows.

4) Timeline risk: 14-day full artifact delivery assumes no legal gating from our downstream vendors. If a vendor requires additional review, we should surface this to Helix immediately and propose a temporary mitigant (e.g., stricter routing to dedicated onshore pool).

- Naomi

title: Procurement path & security gating — MediTech follow-up
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@meditech.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: Procurement path & security gating — MediTech follow-up

Hi Samir — thanks again for the conversation earlier. Quick recap + proposed next steps so we stay aligned: 

1) Summary of ask: MediTech is evaluating Redwood for clinical note generation at scale. Key gating items you flagged: preferred purchase flow (PO vs card vs marketplace), SOC 2 evidence and DPA, and an outstanding security questionnaire from your procurement team.

2) Procurement options we can support:
   - Standard PO/invoice + monthly billing (preferred for enterprise teams)
   - Corporate card (fastest to start, lower paperwork)
   - Cloud marketplace procurement (Azure Marketplace or GCP Marketplace) — useful if you want consolidated cloud billing and marketplace contract terms

3) Security/compliance: we can provide SOC 2 Type II report, a DPA template, and a completed security questionnaire. We typically deliver SOC 2 artifacts under an NDA or post-LOE; DPA is straightforward to execute when the SOW/contract is in place.

4) Proposal for next steps:
   - Samir: confirm preferred procurement route (PO vs card vs marketplace) and whether Procurement needs a vendor form or SSO/SCIM setup details.
   - Lydia/CISO: if possible, share your priority security questions (we can prioritize responses in 48–72 hours).
   - Marissa: we will prepare a draft SOW and PO-friendly invoice schedule once route is confirmed.

Attachments: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2026.pdf (SOC 2 summary), Redwood_DPA_Summary_2026.pdf (DPA highlights).

Happy to hop on a short call if that helps.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

[Attachment: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2026.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: Redwood_DPA_Summary_2026.pdf (pdf)]

From: Samir Patel <samir@meditech.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: MediTech Procurement <procurement@meditech.com>, Lydia Shaw <lydia.shaw@meditech.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 15:48:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Procurement path & security gating — MediTech follow-up

Thanks Ava — helpful recap. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Procurement route: our finance team prefers PO for anything over $50k ARR; shorter pilots (<$10k) can go on card. For enterprise rollout we want PO + invoice.

- Marketplace: we are evaluating Azure Marketplace for other vendors but that adds ~6–8 weeks to legal review; not ideal for a pilot.

- Security: Lydia (CISO) will own the questionnaire. Lydia — I’m looping you in; you’ll see the procurement template attached. Is SOC 2 Type II sufficient to clear the legal/compliance bar for a pilot? If not, what additional artifacts do you need (pen test results, infra diagram, data retention policy)?

We also need a vendor form (W-9/EIN, business address, bank wiring instructions) for finance — can you confirm what you’ll provide and the expected turnaround?

Best,
Samir
VP, IT Procurement | MediTech Health
samir@meditech.com

Hi Samir — Karthik from Commercial Ops here. Quick answers to your items: 

- Vendor form & finance paperwork: we can return a filled vendor pack within 48 business hours once you share the form. We’ll include W-9, EIN, and bank wiring instructions in a secured ZIP.

- SOC 2 / security artifacts: SOC 2 Type II report + DPA is our standard package and is usually sufficient to clear pilot-level procurement. If Lydia wants pen-test artifacts or a VPC architecture diagram, we can provide a redacted infra diagram and a summary of our pentest cadence (and provide a report under an NDA).

- Timeline: PO start to production onboarding usually takes 2–4 weeks (depending on signature and SSO setup). Card-based pilots can start in 2–3 business days. Marketplace adds ~4–8 weeks.

- Billing cadence: we support monthly invoices net-30 by default for PO customers. Annual prepay is also supported for committed capacity.

Next best step: if PO is the chosen route, send the PO template and we’ll provide a formal quote and draft SOW for signature. I’ll also drop a DocuSign link for the DPA if legal prefers that channel.

Regards,
Karthik Iyer

title: Role scope handoff playbook and comms for upcoming team restructure
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Role scope handoff playbook and comms for upcoming team restructure

Team —

Sharing an initial playbook for role scope handoffs tied to the restructure we discussed in last week's leadership sync. Primary goals: preserve customer-facing SLAs, minimize confusion in performance conversations, and keep any sensitive HR language confined to People Ops.

Attached: RoleScope_Matrix.xlsx (owner/coverage per role), Manager_Notes.pdf (talking points for 1:1s), Draft_Announcement.docx (internal announcement draft).

Asks:
- Can each of you review the matrix and flag any role overlaps or critical coverage gaps by EOD Monday?
- Jonas — please surface any technical dependencies that need separate owner transition plans.
- Monica — I want your read on the Draft_Announcement wording; we need neutral, coaching-focused language for performance-related changes and a clear timeline. Do not include any legal or severance specifics in the announcement.

Sensitive note: until we finalize wording with People Ops, keep comms strictly internal leadership -> managers. No broader distribution.

Next step: I'll consolidate comments and propose manager-level calibration sessions (30–45 min) before we send team-level messages.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Platform Product
Redwood Inference

--
attachments: RoleScope_Matrix.xlsx, Manager_Notes.pdf, Draft_Announcement.docx

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 11:34:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Role scope handoff playbook and comms for upcoming team restructure

Ben —

Thanks for pulling this together. Quick inline thoughts: 

> RoleScope_Matrix.xlsx: owner/coverage per role

- Lines 8–12 show potential overlap between Runtime Lead and Serving Lead for batch inference. Suggest splitting \"batch optimization\" as a separate workstream owner for the first 90 days to avoid single-person bottlenecks.
- Add a row for KV-cache migration (Q1) — that has downstream impacts on latency SLOs and requires a temporary cross-pod rotation.

> Manager_Notes.pdf

- Good guidance. Add a short script for managers to use when asked about \"performance\" vs \"scope change\" — e.g., \"This is a scope re-alignment to meet product needs; performance feedback will be provided separately in your upcoming review conversation.\"

Operational ask: can we include a 2-week ramp overlap for senior engineers flagged in the matrix (column F)? For *their* work continuity we should budget a short handoff window rather than an instant cutover.

If you want, I can update the matrix with suggested rows and send v2 by Friday.

—Jonas

title: Evidence mailbag: Bluemint onboarding telemetry and SLA checklist
preview:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@bluemint.io>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Evidence mailbag: Bluemint onboarding telemetry and SLA checklist

Hi Jordan / Monica,

Thanks again for the walkthrough earlier this week. Our infra lead (cc'd) asked I share the initial small-run telemetry we captured when we exercised the Redwood endpoint from our staging region yesterday — attaching the CSV and a JSON snapshot.

Quick context: the run was ~2k tokens per request, steady load ~60 qps for 15 minutes. We saw a P95 that drifted to ~420ms and a few tail spikes around 720ms; not sure if that's noisy from our side or indicative of settings we should change on the Redwood side.

Request: can you confirm what a minimal evidence package for onboarding signoff looks like from your side (latency/throughput targets, required telemetry, accepted file formats)? If you need a fuller run we can schedule one next week — let us know preferred window.

Attachments:
- bluemint_load_run_20261120.csv (text/csv)
- telemetry_snapshot_20261120.json (application/json)

Thanks,
Elena Ruiz
Senior SRE, Bluemint Labs
elena.ruiz@bluemint.io



From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@bluemint.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Evidence mailbag: Bluemint onboarding telemetry and SLA checklist

Elena — thanks for the artifacts, very helpful. Quick read: the CSV shows steady concurrency but a few >P95 spikes that line up with cold KV cache misses in the snapshot. That can happen on short runs.

Proposed minimal evidence package for Bluemint signoff (what we'll deliver):
- 1x confirmed throughput run (30m) at your target QPS with per-request latencies (CSV)
- Telemetry snapshot including KV cache hit rate, GPU utilization, token distribution (JSON)
- Annotated tail-log (plain text) highlighting any observed anomalies
- SLA checklist doc (spreadsheet) showing measured P50/P95/P99 vs target and gating flags

We can produce those artifacts and drop them into the shared Drive folder. Suggested next steps:
- You confirm target throughput and latency gates (example below)
- We schedule a 30-minute controlled run on Tue/Wed next week (we can run in your staging region)
- We run canary, capture artifacts, and we (Redwood) assemble the acceptance packet for signoff

Suggested example gates (open to adjustments):
- Throughput: sustained 120 qps for 30 minutes
- Latency: P95 <= 300ms, P99 <= 650ms
- Error rate: <0.1% request errors

I attached a working SLA checklist (v0.2) and a short runbook for the canary steps to this thread. If those targets are too aggressive we can iterate. Monica / Karthik — can you confirm who will own the run and telemetry capture on Redwood's side?

Thanks — Jordan
Jordan Blake
Technical Account Manager, Redwood Inference
jordan_blake@redwood.com



From: Carlos Mendes <c.mendes@bluemint.io>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@bluemint.io>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 08:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Evidence mailbag: Bluemint onboarding telemetry and SLA checklist

Jordan / Elena — quick add from our engineering lead: Bluemint's primary goal is to validate production-like traffic from our east-us region. Our target (for M0) is 120 qps sustained with P95 under 300ms — if the P95 needs to be 250ms we'd escalate, but 300ms is acceptable.

Also: we prefer runs in the 03:00–07:00 UTC window to match low-traffic windows on our side. We'll co-ordinate SSH access for the synthetic runner if Redwood needs to fetch client-side traces.

Carlos
Carlos Mendes
Platform Manager, Bluemint Labs
c.mendes@bluemint.io



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 11:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Evidence mailbag: Bluemint onboarding telemetry and SLA checklist

Jordan / Monica — looping in Neha for telemetry intake and to confirm RX/TX of artifacts. Proposed internal run plan (Redwood-owned):

1) Prep (Day -1): provision a dedicated canary slot in us-east, pre-warm KV cache with a 15m ramp to minimize cold-miss impact.
2) Canary run (Day 0): 30m sustained at 120 qps, run three identical seeds, collect per-request timing, GPU utilization, and kernel selection logs.
3) Post-run (Day 0 + 1): assemble acceptance packet (CSV + JSON + annotated logs + SLA checklist), upload to shared Drive, and circulate to Bluemint for review.

I attached a short runbook (runbook-canary-steps.pdf) and the SLA checklist draft. I'll own the run execution; Neha will own telemetry collection and artifact assembly. Monica — please own the customer-facing signoff and tracking in HS.

On schedule: Tue 2026-11-30 04:00–05:00 UTC (that maps to early US evening for us); Jordan, Monica — is that workable?

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Senior SRE / Onboarding Eng, Redwood Inference
karthik_iyer@redwood.com



From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@bluemint.io>, Carlos Mendes <c.mendes@bluemint.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 15:48:00 -0800

- telemetry_snapshot_YYYYMMDD.json (KV cache, GPU util, kernel map)
- annotated-tail-run-logs.txt (where we call out anomalies)
- sla-checklist-v0.2.xlsx (measured vs gates, pass/fail flags)

Schedule: Tue 2026-11-30 04:00–04:40 UTC (30m run + 10m buffer). Redwood will pre-warm at 03:45 UTC. We'll upload artifacts to the Drive folder and drop a HubSpot note (HS-234512) once upload completes.

Links / Next actions:
- Shared artifacts folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB_example_shared_run_artifacts

- HubSpot record: HS-234512 (I'll log artifacts and status)

title: Activation gating for AWS Private Offers: buyer banding & subscription tiers
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-11-25T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Team —

Following the call this morning with AcmeCo and Carlos from AWS, I sketched a lightweight banding approach we can use across private offers to accelerate buyer approvals and co-sell activation. Attached: po_banding_matrix_v1.xlsx and a short activation playbook draft.

Key proposal (high-level):
- Band A: <$100k ARR equivalent, auto-approval via Seller Ops + one finance item (invoice cadence).
- Band B: $100k–$500k, requires seller manager + finance quick review (24–48h) and AWS partner signoff for co-sell.
- Band C: >$500k, full procurement evidence pack + legal/finance signoff and DocuSign.

Questions I want us to decide on in the next 48 hours:
1) Exact thresholds (USD / ARR equivalent) — are the breakpoints above reasonable?
2) Minimal evidence per band (what must live in the HubSpot deal to trigger activation).
3) Owner for automated gating (PR/Partner Ops vs Seller Ops) and whether we route to finance first for Band B.

Action items: please review the attached matrix and the playbook. Vanessa, can you confirm AWS co-sell expectations? Marcus, can you own the gating automation checklist? Priya, can you draft the evidence template for finance?

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: po_banding_matrix_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), aws_activation_playbook_draft.pdf (pdf)


From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Ben — great framing. A few notes from AWS partner side after syncing with Carlos: 
- AWS wants a clear activation SLA for co-sell lead assignment — recommend 72h for Band A/B to assign partner rep and 5 business days for Band C.
- For Band B, a one-pager (commercial summary + contact list) + confirmation of buyer identity is usually sufficient for initial co-sell allocation.
- Band C will need the procurement packet (SCA, billing contact, PO window) before we push co-sell resources.

I'll loop Carlos into the next doc review. I like the automated gating idea; we should be careful not to increase friction for high-velocity SMB deals.

Vanessa Ortiz
Global Marketplace Lead | Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T13:20:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Thanks — reviewed the matrix. Technical notes for gating automation:
- HubSpot deal properties we can use as triggers: \"Estimated ARR\", \"PO Expected Value\", \"Primary Billing Contact\", \"Procurement Contact Email\".
- For Band A, implement an automated workflow that sets \"PO Activation Ready\" when Estimated ARR < 100000 and Billing Contact verified.
- For Band B, add a two-step: auto-notify finance with one-click \"Approve\" in HubSpot that sets a 48h timer. If finance doesn't approve, escalate to Seller Ops.

I'll draft the checklist in Confluence and link it in the playbook — will aim to have the automation spec by Wednesday (2026-11-28).

Marcus Lin
Head of Seller Ops | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com


From: Lisa Tran <lisa.tran@acmeco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-26T09:07:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Proposed buyer-banding approach to speed AWS PO approvals (AcmeCo)

Ben / Vanessa — thanks for the clear summary. A couple of quick points from AcmeCo procurement: 
- We prefer Band B behavior: we can move to a 12-month usage commitment with quarterly invoicing; finance will need a short evidence pack (signed SOW or summary email + procurement contact).
- For Band C (if we expand later) procurement will request our legal team to review SLA and data retention.

Happy to review the one-pager once you have it.

—Lisa

title: Renewal conversation — FoxLane competitive evaluation
preview:
From: Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@foxlane.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@foxlane.com
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Renewal window + benchmarking request

Ben —

Our renewal for the FoxLane inference footprint is coming up in ~30 days and I wanted to flag a competitive process we've just accelerated. NimbusAI has approached our SREs with a performance benchmark offer — they claim sub-120ms 99th on our gpt-4-style workload at 8k context. That's catching attention because we are planning to expand from 4 GPUs to ~12 GPUs to support a new personalization pilot.

Before we go too far with them, procurement asked if Redwood can:
- Run an impartial 48-hour benchmark using our sample traces (attached RFP),
- Provide a commercial alternative if results are comparable (pricing/commitments),
- Confirm whether you can guarantee latency SLO adjustments for a 12-GPU dedicated pool.

This is high priority — if Nimbus proves the numbers and gives us a competitive commercial, we will have to include them in the short list. Would appreciate an outline of how Redwood would approach a bake-off and any preliminary commercial flexibility you can share.

Thanks,
Samantha Reed
Head of Infrastructure, FoxLane
Samantha Reed
Attachment: FoxLane_RFP_2026.pdf (application/pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@foxlane.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 10:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Renewal window + benchmarking request

Samantha —

Thanks for the heads up and the RFP. We can absolutely run an impartial 48-hour benchmark in our lab against the traces you provide. High-level plan:

- We will ingest a sanitized sample of your production traces (you can use the attached bench_request_form to indicate traffic slices and goals).
- Redwood will run the workload across three model+quantization variants and a 12-GPU dedicated pool configured to mirror your expected capacity.
- We will report latency percentiles (50/95/99), throughput, and per-token cost.

To make the benchmark fair we will need the trace bundle and a short list of the top 10 prompt templates. If you want, Karthik (copied) can run the technical kickoff and provide a runbook.

Commercially, we can propose a Dedicated pool commitment for the expanded capacity. I don't want to pre-commit to discounts without checking with my manager, but we can outline a firm offer tied to a 12-month commit and a short onboarding credit.

Attached: bench_request_form.pdf (fill and return)

Propose we schedule the technical kickoff this week — Karthik, are you available?

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
Cell: +1 (415) 555-0142

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 14:30:00 -0800
Subject: FW: Renewal window + benchmarking request — FoxLane (competitive threat)

Team — forwarding FoxLane's RFP and request for a 48-hour impartial benchmark. Key points: renewal in ~30 days, looking to expand to ~12 GPUs, NimbusAI in the deal.

Options I'm considering to defend/expand:
1) Standard renewal: 12-month renewal at list, no onboarding credits — higher churn risk.
2) Dedicated commit offer: 12 GPU dedicated pool, 10% off list for committed term, $50k one-time onboarding credit, and 2 months of burst at no charge to cover initial spin-up and traffic spikes.
3) Premium keep: aggressive discount + extended SLA concessions (need leadership signoff).

Asks:
- Marissa: can you greenlight option (2) pricing-wise? What budget headroom do we have for onboarding credit?
- Neha: if we offer SLA tweaks (exceptions on 99th under 10ms bump for certain tails) who signs off on that? Legal will need to know.
- Karthik: prepare the runbook and estimate run costs for the 48-hour lab (we'll include this as part of the proposal packet).

I've attached a draft counter (proposed_counter.pdf) and the benchmark plan (benchmark_plan.md). If we proceed with (2) I want to send a clean commercial offer to Samantha by Friday.

Thanks — Ben

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Nov 2026 09:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: FW: Renewal window + benchmarking request — FoxLane (competitive threat)

Ben —

We should pursue option (2) as the baseline defend. Approvals/constraints:

- Pricing: approve up to 10% off MRC for a 12-month committed Dedicated pool sized to 12 GPUs.
- Credits: approve a $40k onboarding credit (not $50k) and 2 months of burst capacity at no charge (to cover the pilot).
- SLA adjustments: legal must sign off on any explicit tail-SLA concessions. Neha, please coordinate with Legal — I do not want to make exceptions that set a precedent unless there is a formal SLA appendix.
- Escalation: if FoxLane presses for >$60k credit or >12% discount we escalate to leadership (I will take it).

Karthik — please prepare the runbook and include a short appendix that documents the lab environment parity and what we will measure (latency p50/p95/p99, cost-per-100k tokens). Also include a short statement on how we will isolate the test to ensure impartiality vs Nimbus (e.g., signed test plan).

Ben — once you get the trace bundle, schedule the kickoff with Karthik and I'll draft the commercial template.

— Marissa
VP, Sales — Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@foxlane.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@foxlane.com
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 15:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Renewal window + benchmarking request

Samantha —

Thanks again for sending the RFP. Quick summary of what Redwood can commit to so you have something to circulate internally:

1) Benchmark: 48-hour impartial run using your trace bundle and top prompt templates. Karthik will run the kickoff and deliver an independent report with latency (p50/p95/p99), throughput, and cost metrics.

2) Commercial: Dedicated 12-GPU pool with a 12-month committed term. Commercial highlights: 10% off monthly recurring charges, $40,000 one-time onboarding credit, and two months of burst capacity at no charge. This offer is tied to a signed commitment by FoxLane within 21 days of proposal delivery.

3) SLA & legal: Any explicit tail-SLA exceptions require legal review and a signed appendix. We will include standard SLA language in the proposal; if you require deviations we'll surface them for approval.

Attached: redwood_proposal_v2.pdf, bench_results_template.xlsx
DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/FL-2026-Proposal-Redwood
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/1234567/FL-2026-Q4-RENEW

Next steps:
- Please share the sanitized trace bundle and top prompt templates (use the bench_request_form).
- We will schedule the technical kickoff within 48 hours of receiving the bundle.
- Karthik will run the benchmark and deliver results; if we meet the agreed parity targets we will finalize the commercial package and route to DocuSign.

Quote from your initial note: 

> "NimbusAI has approached our SREs with a performance benchmark offer — they claim sub-120ms 99th on our gpt-4-style workload at 8k context."

We welcome the head-to-head. Our approach is transparent; the test plan will be mutually agreed so results are acceptable to procurement.

If anything above needs tweaking, flag it and we'll adjust quickly.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference

title: Post-call: Retrieval pipeline & Reranker recommendations for Veridian AI
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ella Brooks <ella@veridian.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call recap — retrieval pipeline recommendations and next steps

Ella / Tom — thanks again for the time earlier. Quick recap from our call and the concrete next steps we discussed so everyone is aligned.

Summary of asks:
- Run a focused RAG pilot against your product support corpus (approx 120k docs) to validate grounding and answer accuracy for end-user queries.
- Evaluate two retrieval configurations + two reranker candidates and compare grounding metrics vs. your current baseline.
- Share a prompt set (30 representative queries) and 200 sample docs for a warm-start evaluation.

Redwood’s recommended starting configuration (what we propose to spin up for the pilot):
- Retriever: hybrid (dense embeddings + BM25) with Weaviate vector index. Embedding model: rw-embed-2 (256d).
- Chunking: 512-token chunks, 150-token sliding overlap (to preserve context across long docs).
- Scoring: combine vector cosine score (weight 0.7) + BM25 (weight 0.3); threshold: cosine >= 0.78 for consideration.
- Reranker candidates: (A) cross-encoder-deberta-v3-large (highest fidelity, cross-encoder) and (B) sentence-transformer-distilroberta-base-msmarco (lightweight re-rank).
- Caching: KV cache enabled for prefix answers and 60s short-term retrieval cache for repeated queries in a session.

Grounding / quality metrics we’ll report:
- Citation precision (proportion of model answers that include accurate citations) — target > 92%
- Evidence recall (how often the cited docs contain the asserted fact) — target > 88%
- Hallucination rate (assertions without supporting docs) — target < 3%
- Latency (end-to-end 95th percentile) — target < 350ms for single-question retrieval + rerank pipeline on dedicated capacity

Deliverables and timeline (proposed):
1) By 11/30 — you send: prompt set (30 queries) + 200 sample docs + any redlines for data security.
2) 12/1–12/6 — Redwood runs evaluation of 2 retriever configs x 2 rerankers (4 experiments) and produces baseline metrics + short error analysis.
3) 12/8 — 30 min sync to review results and agree on pilot SOW.

Attachments/links: eval_prompt_set_v1.json (attached), sample_docs.tar.gz (attached), shared benchmark folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cVeridianEval

Next steps for you: please confirm you can share the 200 sample docs by 11/30 and whether procurement needs an SOW before the evaluation work begins.

Thanks — I’ll coordinate the Redwood side and loop in Marissa & Monica for the evaluation execution.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0132

-----
Attachment: eval_prompt_set_v1.json (application/json)
Attachment: sample_docs.tar.gz (application/gzip)

2) For the reranker compute, what's the expected incremental cost if we run cross-encoder-deberta-v3-large vs the distilroberta option? Ballpark numbers are fine.

We can share the 200 sample docs by 11/30. Procurement asked that any SOW include a short paragraph on data handling and audit logs. I attached an initial SOW request (PDF) to this thread.

—Ella

Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 14:00:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Fwd: Post-call recap — retrieval pipeline recommendations and next steps

Ben / Marissa — will do. Quick notes from the infra side: 
- We can run rw-embed-2 in Veridian's VPC via our Private deployment package to meet the non-exfiltration requirement; embeddings will be generated inside the customer VPC and only vector fingerprints (not raw docs) will be stored if they request that. I'll include the exact KMS/HSM integration notes in the SOW paragraph.
- For the experiments, I propose we also include a higher-chunk config (1,024 token chunks with 200 overlap) as an A/B to see if bigger chunks reduce hallucination on long procedural docs. That would make 6 experiments total (3 chunk configs x 2 rerankers). If budget is a concern, we can drop the distil reranker for the larger-chunk runs.
- Cost estimate: cross-encoder run for full 30-query set + 200 docs (re-ranking top-20 candidates) will consume approx 12 GPU-minutes per experiment on our dedicated nodes; distil reranker is ~2.5 GPU-minutes. I’ll add a short cost table to the experiment run notes.

I’ll start the runs as soon as we have the sample docs.

—Monica

From: Alex Johnson <alex@fluxretail.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@fluxretail.com
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0500
Subject: Intro + quick ask — demo for production evaluation

Hi Chloe,

We signed up for Redwood earlier this month (self-serve) and have been experimenting with the hosted API for our catalog enrichment flows. The trial went well and engineering wants a short demo focused on:
- handling ~200k inference requests/day (mostly short generation + reranking)
- concurrency spikes during ET peak hours (estimated 120 qps at peak)
- data residency / audit log questions for compliance

Can we get a 30–40 minute walkthrough this week? Procurement is copied to start any contract steps if the demo looks good.

Prefer Eastern time. Available windows: Thu/Fri mornings ET.

Thanks,
Alex Johnson
Head of Integrations, Flux Retail
alex@fluxretail.com
Attachment: flux-retail-infra-notes.txt (text)


From: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex@fluxretail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Intro + quick ask — demo for production evaluation

Hi Alex — great to hear the self-serve tests went well and thanks for the details. Quick set of clarifying questions so we tailor the session: 

1) Traffic shape: is the 200k requests/day steady or is it concentrated in a 4–6 hour window? What % are short generations vs. embeddings/reranks?
2) Latency SLO: do you have an internal target (p95 or p99) we should aim to show?
3) Data controls: you mentioned audit logs — do you need SOC2-level artifacts or more around per-request retention/forwarding controls?
4) Pilot timeline: are you evaluating for a Q1 production go-live, or is this exploratory?
5) Demo logistics: we can do 30 minutes focused on architecture and perf + 10 minutes for Q&A. Which days/times this week work best for you?

Propose three 30–40 minute slots (ET):
• Thu Nov 26 — 10:00–10:40 AM ET
• Thu Nov 26 — 2:00–2:40 PM ET
• Fri Nov 27 — 9:30–10:10 AM ET

Ben (SDR) — looping you in to coordinate calendar and share a short pre-demo checklist. Monica (AE) — feel free to join for the pricing/contract Q&A.

Also attaching a one-pager that covers throughput curves for similar retail customers and a simple cost model — should help procurement.

Best,
Chloe Martin
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
chloe@redwood.com
Redwood Inference — Production-first inference platform
Attachments: flux-retail-demo-brief.pdf (PDF), proposed-malp-usage-matrix.xlsx (Excel)

To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@fluxretail.com
Date: Thu, 26 Nov 2026 08:47:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Intro + quick ask — demo for production evaluation

Hey Chloe — thanks for the quick follow-up. Answers inline: 

1) Traffic shape: ~60% of the daily load is concentrated between 10:00–14:00 ET during promotions; outside of that it's fairly low. The mix is ~70% short generation (product title/snippet), ~25% rerank calls, ~5% embeddings for analytics.
2) Latency SLO: engineering would like p95 < 200ms for short gen and p99 < 400ms ideally. We understand these will change with scale but want to see realistic knobs.
3) Data controls: procurement will need SOC2 artifacts and a basic outline of per-request retention and log export options.
4) Pilot timeline: aiming for a January pilot (Q1) with a potential production rollout mid-Q2, pending procurement.
5) Demo logistics: any of your proposed slots work, but Fri Nov 27 at 9:30 AM ET is best for our team.

We can share a short doc with traffic samples if that helps the prep.

—Alex

From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Zhou <ezhou@fastretail.com>
Cc: FastRetail Procurement <procurement@fastretail.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 14:37:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: short-term concurrency increase for launch test

Ethan —

Thanks — we can certainly support a temporary concurrency exception. I’ll coordinate internally and get you a concise proposal (price + cap + invoicing path) by tomorrow EOD PST.

High-level options we typically offer:
- Short-term headroom: fixed flat fee for the window + standard overrun rate (pro-rated).
- True-up approach: grant provisional headroom, invoice for the uplift at an agreed flat blended rate, reconcile after the event.
- Commit swap: if you commit to an extra month of elevated capacity we can apply a partial discount to the uplift or cap the overrun.

I’ll include a pro forma quote and a redline-ready contract snippet for Procurement.

Camila Reyes
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0202

4) Finance: how should we invoice? Jordan, can we do a proforma invoice to attach to their PO and then true-up on the monthly invoice?

Action items:
- Ben: produce 2 price options (conservative & aggressive) and a proforma quote by tomorrow morning PST.
- Markus: quick redline and note on any legal/contractual risk.
- Jordan: confirm invoicing flow and whether we can apply a temporary cap in billing system.

Camila

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: short-term concurrency increase for launch test — pricing & approvals needed

Camila / team —

Draft numbers and recommendation below (aimed at balancing predictability for FastRetail and protecting Redwood from runaway exposure):

Option A — Predictable flat uplift (recommended)- 72-hour temporary double concurrency: flat fee $5,500 (covers baseline extra capacity).
- Overrun rate: $0.095 per token for first 48 hours, then $0.16 per token thereafter.
- Hard cap during window: $12,000 total.
- Benefit: Procurement gets a clean number for the PO; we avoid large month-end surprises.

Option B — Lower upfront, higher variable (cost-containment for Redwood)
- 72-hour provisional headroom: $2,500 flat fee.
- Overrun: $0.06 per token for the first X tokens (up to a threshold), then $0.20/token beyond.
- No cap (or very high cap). Not preferred from procurement perspective.

My pick: Option A paired with a single-line addendum. Attachments: proforma quote (FR-2381_proforma-quote.pdf) and a redline (FR-2381_contract-redline.docx) — Markus, please review redline. Jordan, confirm we can issue a proforma invoice and then true-up on Dec invoice.
